project.name: example-app
rules: Clean cutover: delete obsolete code; no backfill/dual-read/compat shims unless the task says so; clean stale code first, then implement.
Match the surrounding code style; reuse existing helpers and patterns instead of inventing parallel ones.
All writes go through the audited write path — never write tenant-scoped tables directly.
Keep the build, typecheck, and existing tests green at every stage.
environment: Dev URLs (local only): API http://localhost:4000, web http://localhost:3000.
forbidden: NEVER trigger a deploy or touch shared/staging/production infrastructure or databases.
NEVER verify against staging/production URLs — local dev servers only.
implementationNotes: Migrations: from the db package run `npx prisma migrate dev --name <snake_case>` (DB is up), then regenerate the client with `npm run -w packages/db generate`.
Typecheck each touched package with `npx tsc --noEmit` (or the package's `npm run typecheck`).

reconAreas.prompt: Map the API/service/persistence code this task touches: the relevant modules under apps/api/src/, the data layer (Prisma schema + access helpers). State current behavior with file:line refs and how it differs from the target for THIS task.
Map the API surface this task touches: routes/endpoints/handlers and their input/output shapes; which exist, which are missing.
Map the web frontend scope: which routes/pages/components show or edit the data this task changes, and how they call the API. If the UI is untouched by this task, say so explicitly.
Map the test patterns: where API/integration + database tests live and how they are run; the Playwright/UI setup; and existing fixtures/seeds useful for THIS task.

staticChecks.label: lint
staticChecks.command: `npm run lint` at the repo root (no --fix).

staticChecks.label: typecheck
staticChecks.command: Typecheck every package this task touched: `npx tsc --noEmit` in each.

testing.api: Author integration tests under apps/api that exercise the affected endpoints with the repo's runner (e.g. `npm test -- <file>`). Assert success responses AND error/edge cases (validation, auth, not-found, conflict).
testing.database: In the same test, assert the resulting DB state directly: rows created/updated/soft-deleted, audit/log rows written, and no drift between the API response and the persisted record.
testing.ui: Drive the affected UI flow in a REAL browser via the Playwright MCP (load the browser tools via ToolSearch): navigate to the page, log in, click/type through the flow, and browser_take_screenshot the before/after states (save under .playwright-mcp/); assert both the on-screen result and the DB side-effect (query the local DB). Also author a durable headless Playwright regression spec under tests/e2e/ following an existing one for login helpers + selectors. Requires dev servers up.

docs: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — update when modules, data flow, or schema change.
README.md — update setup/usage when commands or env change.
invariants: Every mutation is written through the audited write path so there is BOTH a change-log row AND a matching update to the materialized record, with the live value equal to the row's after-value — no record write without a log row, no log row without the record reflecting it.
Every delete is a SOFT delete: a change-log delete row is appended (the before-value preserves the record) and the record stops surfacing — no orphaned rows still showing.
Any new tenant-scoped table has a row-level-security policy added AND applied.
